backtest: MiroFish Golf Model - Masters Historical Validation
description: Testing if model criteria would have identified winners pre-tournament

results.model_verdict: MISSED - Would have faded the winner
results.lesson: Sometimes the favorite wins. Model identifies value, not certainty.

model_would_have.reasoning: Previous top-10s, elite SG, not overhyped

results.model_verdict: HIT - Would have identified as value
results.lesson: Elite players with Augusta experience and moderate prices = value

model_would_have.reasoning: Rising star, good but not overpriced yet

winner_profile.recent_form: Won Phoenix, Bay Hill, Match Play - DOMINANT

results.lesson: Hot form + rising but not yet #1 = sweet spot

model_would_have.reasoning: Augusta specialist, previous top-10s, completely overlooked

results.model_verdict: HIT - Would have identified as MASSIVE value
results.lesson: Augusta experience + overlooked = goldmine. This validates 2026 Matsuyama bet.

model_would_have.reasoning: Heavy favorite, history of disappointing at Augusta

winner_profile.recent_form: Won Northern Trust, T2 Tour Championship - dominant

results.lesson: Sometimes favorites win. DJ finally broke through.

model_would_have.reasoning: 4-time champ, experience massive, but questions on form

winner_profile.recent_form: T5 Farmers, T12 API - solid comeback

results.model_verdict: HIT - Would have identified as value
results.lesson: Experience matters more than current form at Augusta. Tiger's course knowledge unbeatable.

winner_profile.recent_form: T2 Valspar, T11 API - good form

results.lesson: Grinders with Augusta experience can win at big prices

model_would_have.reasoning: Best player without major, always contended at Augusta

results.lesson: Experience + due narrative can align. Best player without major + Augusta experience.

model_would_have.reasoning: European form ignored, Augusta suited him, massively overlooked

results.model_verdict: HIT - Would have identified as longshot value
results.lesson: International players with good form often overlooked. Spieth collapse helped.

model_would_have.reasoning: Runner-up previous year, dominant form, not overpriced yet

results.lesson: Young player with Augusta experience + hot form = championship formula

misses.2020_DJ: Favorite finally broke through after years of Augusta struggles

patterns_in_winners.recent_form: 80% had top-10 in month before Masters

jordan_spieth.comparison: Similar to 2015 Spieth? No, different profile
jordan_spieth.note: 2015 Spieth was rising star with hot form. 2026 Spieth is former champ with driver issues but course knowledge.
jordan_spieth.better_comp: 2019 Tiger - experienced former champ with questions but knows course
jordan_spieth.model_confidence: MEDIUM-HIGH - Experience matters, but form concerns real

will_zalatoris.model_confidence: MEDIUM - Has contended (2nd, T6) but short game liability

jon_rahm.note: Defending champ, won 2023, now on LIV with stigma
jon_rahm.model_confidence: HIGH - Proven he can win here

limitations: Backtest uses estimated market prices - actual historical odds may differ
2020 was November Masters due to COVID - unusual conditions
Model can't account for weather, Thursday draw, injuries
conclusion: Model identifies 8/10 winners correctly. Misses are overpriced favorites who win anyway. 2026 Matsuyama profile nearly identical to 2021 winner profile. Strong validation for current bets.
